marginal-p-x.title: Marginal p(x)
marginal-p-x.plain_explanation: The data distribution is a mixture of likelihoods under the latent prior.
marginal-p-x.term_by_term:
- **p(x)**: density of observations.
- **p(x|z)**: how data is generated given latent z.
- **p(z)**: prior over latents.
- **∫ dz**: sum (integrate) over all possible z.
marginal-p-x.intuition_geometric: Each z induces a slice p(x|z); stacking and weighting by p(z) forms the observed cloud of x.
marginal-p-x.code_example:
import numpy as np
# toy: sample z ~ N(0,1), x|z ~ N(z, 0.1)
z = np.random.randn(1000)
x = z + np.random.randn(1000) * 0.1
print(x.mean(), x.std())
